summary: Validation layer is correctly implemented with strict artifact checks, cross-stage consistency enforcement, and deterministic reporting. Implementation satisfies acceptance criteria and adheres to orchestration constraints.
reasons: Validation module explicitly checks required artifacts including timeline.json, translated_transcript.json, dubbed_audio, and final_video using deterministic file resolution via resolveWorkspacePaths.
Dubbed audio duration validation is implemented using WAV parsing (getWavDurationSeconds) and compared against timeline end with tolerance, satisfying duration consistency requirement.
Segment count preservation between timeline and translated_transcript is enforced using getTimelineSegmentCount, with failure conditions handled explicitly.
Timeline consistency validation is implemented via validateTimelineSegmentOrdering, ensuring structural correctness beyond simple existence checks.
Final video validation enforces exactly one audio stream using ffprobeAudioStreamCount, satisfying composition constraint.
Validation report is generated deterministically via stableStringify and written as validation_report.json with structured checks and errors.

Tests (tests/validation.test.js) explicitly inject invalid scenarios (duration mismatch, segment mismatch, multi-audio streams) and confirm pipeline validation fails, aligning with verification steps.
Implementation performs validation only and does not modify or repair artifacts, preserving runner orchestration-only constraint.
All validation logic resides within allowed paths and integrates cleanly with existing workspace and artifact contract system.
